# dokploy_application (Resource)

A Dokploy application sourced from a Docker image. Applying this resource deploys the application and waits for it to finish.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "dokploy_application" "api" {
  environment_id = dokploy_project.app.production_environment_id
  name           = "api"
  docker_image   = "nginx:1.27"

  env = {
    PORT = "8080"
  }

  timeouts {
    create = "15m"
    update = "15m"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `docker_image` (String) Docker image to deploy, e.g. `nginx:1.27`.
- `environment_id` (String) Environment the application belongs to. Changing this forces replacement.
- `name` (String) Application name.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Application description. Note: once set, removing this attribute from the configuration does not clear the description on the server (the API silently keeps the existing value).
- `env` (Map of String) Application environment variables.
- `registry_password` (String, Sensitive) Registry password. Not returned by the API; drift on this field is not detected.
- `registry_url` (String) Private registry URL. Omit for public images.
- `registry_username` (String, Sensitive) Registry username.
- `server_id` (String) Managed server (`dokploy_server.x.id`) the application runs on. Omit to run on the Dokploy host. Changing this forces replacement.
- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))

### Read-Only

- `app_name` (String) Internal application name generated by Dokploy.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `status` (String) Status of the most recent deployment.

<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `update` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import dokploy_application.api <applicationId>
```
